review_notes:
1. I inferred the runtime launcher as a generic MPI launcher placeholder; if the benchmark environment requires `srun` or `mpiexec` instead of `mpirun`, substitute that in the command template.
2. The staged data file already contains 216 waters (648 atoms). After the scripted `replicate 4 4 4`, the benchmark actually runs 13,824 waters / 41,472 atoms; please confirm that this expanded system size is the intended fixed-size target.
3. There is no repo-local test that exactly matches the staged NVE benchmark scripts. The closest correctness references are the TIP4P force-style YAML fixtures and molecular example inputs, so benchmark tolerances should be reviewed by the user before being treated as authoritative.
1. The runner currently enforces thermo observables plus `neighbor_list_builds` and `dangerous_builds`; force-component checks are not encoded because the staged TIP4P scripts do not emit force dumps.
2. The YAML defaults `FERMILINK_GOAL_INPUT_ROOT` to `.fermilink-optimize/inputs/all`, but the runner will honor a real environment override first. Verify the controller sets this to the staged root it actually provides, especially for worker-only runs.
3. MPI launcher autodetection prefers `mpirun`, then `mpiexec`, then `srun`. If the cluster needs a different launcher or extra flags, set `FERMILINK_MPI_LAUNCHER` explicitly.
4. I validated YAML loading and Python syntax, and I executed the runner once; it failed cleanly only because `build/lmp` does not exist yet. The `runtime.pre_commands` are expected to satisfy that before authoritative benchmarking.
